channel.title: Tom M. Ragonneau
channel.description: Recent content on Tom M. Ragonneau

item.title: Wednesday, 18 January 2023
item.description: Title: A Derivative-Free Direct-Search Methods for Nonlinear Optimization. Venue: TU817. Notes This talk introduced a new heterogeneous direct-search method for unconstrained optimization.

item.title: Tuesday, 10 January 2023
item.description: Title: The missing semester course. Venue: Online. Notes This talk introduced the missing semester course taught at MIT. The authors focused on shell tools and scripting, the vim editor, and version control using git.

item.title: Wednesday, 23 November 2022
item.description: Title: The COBYQA method. Venue: TU817, The Hong Kong Polytechnic University. Notes This talk presented the fifth and seventh chapters of my Ph.D. thesis.

item.title: Friday, 18 November 2022
item.description: Title: The SQP method. Venue: TU817, The Hong Kong Polytechnic University. Notes This talk presented the fourth chapter of my Ph.D. thesis.

item.title: Monday, 14 November 2022
item.description: Title: Development of the PDFO package. Venue: TU817, The Hong Kong Polytechnic University. Notes This talk presented the third chapter of my Ph.D. thesis.

item.title: COBYQA
item.description: COBYQA (Constrained Optimization BY Quadratic Approximations) is a derivative-free trust-region SQP optimization solver for constrained optimization using quadratic approximations. It is currently implemented in Python, but a Fortran version of the software (and interfaces for MATLAB and Python) will appear in the future. A complete documentation is available. The source files can be downloaded from GitHub. Compiled versions can be installed from PyPI. A detailed description of the algorithm and its implementation is given in my Ph.

item.title: PDFO
item.description: PDFO (Powell’s Derivative-Free Optimization solvers) is a cross-platform package providing MATLAB and Python interfaces for using late Professor M. J. D. Powell’s derivative-free optimization solvers. It includes UOBYQA, NEWUOA, BOBYQA, LINCOA, and COBYLA. A complete documentation is available. The source files can be downloaded from GitHub. Compiled versions for Python can be installed from PyPI.

item.title: Monday, 26 September 2022
item.description: Title: Interpolation models for derivative free optimization. Venue: TU717, The Hong Kong Polytechnic University. Notes This talk presented the second chapter of my Ph.D. thesis.

item.title: Tuesday, 13 September 2022
item.description: Title: Overview of derivative free optimization. Venue: TU717, The Hong Kong Polytechnic University. Notes This talk presented the first chapter of my Ph.D. thesis.

item.title: Kantorovich inequality
item.description: Let $A \in \mathbb{R}^{n \times n}$ be a given symmetric positive definite matrix and denote its eigenvalues $\lambda_1 \le \lambda_2 \le \dots \le \lambda_n$. The Kantorovich inequality states that for all vector $x \in \mathbb{R}^n$ of unit norm, we have $$ \langle x, A x \rangle \langle x, A^{-1} x \rangle \le \frac{(\lambda_1 + \lambda_n)^2}{4 \lambda_1 \lambda_n}.$$ Such an inequality is widely used in convergence analysis (e.g., the convergence rate of the steepest descent method can be derived from the Kantorovich inequality).
